evals.prompt: Write a spec for an actor that watches a directory for new CSV files, validates their schema against a known set of expected columns, and moves valid files to an output directory. Invalid files should be moved to a quarantine directory with a rejection report.
evals.expected_output: Actor spec with Purpose, name-only ## Receives, Workflow; specs/messages.md with full message signatures; imperative internal steps (no self-mailbox fiction); every outbound message uses reply to `replyTo` or Spawn the Subagent; conditional valid/invalid paths.

assertions.text: Has '# ... Actor Specification' title heading
Has '## Actor Purpose' section
Has '## Receives' section (name-only inbound messages; no separate Sends section)
Has '## Workflow' section with numbered steps
Under ## Receives in the actor spec, each line lists only a message name in backticks (e.g. `- `MessageName`); full signatures and glosses belong in specs/messages.md
Includes or references specs/messages.md (or equivalent) with full `- `Signature` - gloss` lines for each protocol message
messages.md (or the catalog) uses Scala stdlib types where appropriate, Java types fully qualified when used, no external library types, no unnecessary single-field wrapper types
Workflow does not use the word 'emit' (outbound sends use only reply to `replyTo` with ... and/or Spawn the Subagent [Name](path))
Workflow does not use bare 'reply with `Message(...)`' outside the permitted patterns; outbound sends use reply to `replyTo` with ... and/or Spawn the Subagent [Name](path)
## Receives lists only inter-actor messages (not internal step labels masquerading as messages); no fake self-mailbox protocol for sequential work in the same actor
Uses second-person voice ('Your purpose is to...' or 'You...')

Definitions completeness: if any `_Term_` appears in Actor Purpose or Workflow, then either (a) `## Definitions` after `## Suite references` lists every such term with a gloss, or (b) `## Suite references` includes a link to a shared `specs/*.md` definitions file that defines every such term
Has conditional branching (valid vs invalid file handling)
Actor spec is under 100 lines

evals.prompt: I need a spec for an orchestrator that coordinates three workers to process a batch of images — resizing, watermarking, and uploading — with up to 2 retries per image if any worker fails.
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator spec plus three subagent specs; specs/messages.md; name-only Receives; each Spawn the Subagent step states what the child does, expected reply messages, and per-reply branches; bounded retry; explicit reply to `replyTo` and Spawn the Subagent where needed.

assertions.text: Main spec has '# ... Actor Specification' title heading
Main spec has '## Receives' (name-only inbound messages; no separate Sends section)
Main spec uses 'Spawn the Subagent [Name](path)' pattern with markdown links for child actors

Each Spawn the Subagent step describes what the child does and which reply messages the parent expects, with nested steps for each reply branch (not merely which request/response types the child handles)
Has explicit retry bound (up to 2 retries / 3 attempts)
Creates separate subagent spec files for workers (not inline)
Under ## Receives in each actor spec, only message names in backticks appear; full definitions live in specs/messages.md
messages.md catalogs message signatures with Scala stdlib types where appropriate and fully qualified Java types when used
Workflow does not use 'emit'; outbound sends use only reply to `replyTo` with ... and/or Spawn the Subagent [Name](path)
Uses second-person voice

Definitions completeness: in orchestrator and each worker spec, if any `_Term_` appears in Actor Purpose or Workflow, then that file either has inline `## Definitions` after `## Suite references` listing every term, or `## Suite references` links a shared `specs/*.md` definitions file that defines every such term
Each actor spec is under 100 lines

evals.prompt: Create a spec for an actor that receives a PR diff and uses an LLM to generate a code review, then posts the review as GitHub comments. The actor should first analyze the diff to identify files worth reviewing, then spawn an LLM-based reviewer subagent for the actual review generation, and finally post the comments via the GitHub API.
evals.expected_output: Main actor spec plus LLM reviewer subagent; specs/messages.md; spawn step with expected replies and branches; (Agentic Step) where needed; every outbound message uses reply to `replyTo` or Spawn the Subagent.

assertions.text: Main spec has '# ... Actor Specification' title heading
Main spec has '## Receives' (name-only inbound messages; no separate Sends section)
Main spec uses 'Spawn the Subagent [Name](path)' pattern for LLM reviewer with nested reply branches (what the child returns and what the parent does on each)
Identifies the LLM step as delegated to a child actor (not done inline)
Workflow steps that are LLM/agentic (including subagent delegation for review generation) start with '(Agentic Step)'
Creates a separate subagent spec file for the LLM reviewer
messages.md (or referenced catalog) defines protocol messages with appropriate types; actor ## Receives is name-only
Workflow does not use 'emit'; outbound sends use only reply to `replyTo` with ... and/or Spawn the Subagent [Name](path)
Uses second-person voice

Definitions completeness: in main orchestrator and LLM reviewer specs, if any `_Term_` appears in Actor Purpose or Workflow, then that file either has inline `## Definitions` after `## Suite references` listing every term, or `## Suite references` links a shared `specs/*.md` definitions file that defines every such term
Has conditional branching for API failure handling
Each actor spec is under 100 lines
